# Origin

> Origin is local-first memory for AI work. Hybrid retrieval (93.6% Recall@5 on LongMemEval oracle, 70.0% on LoCoMo), real git versioning of every memory write in ~/.origin/.git/, mandatory provenance on distilled wiki pages, and one daemon serving Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Claude Desktop, Gemini CLI, and other MCP-compatible AI tools. Cross-platform on macOS (arm64, x64), Linux (x86_64, aarch64; glibc), and Windows (x86_64). Apache-2.0. Source at github.com/7xuanlu/origin.

## Core

- [Origin homepage](https://useorigin.app/): What Origin is, who it is for, how it differs from cloud memory layers.
- [About](https://useorigin.app/about): Project background and the person behind Origin.
- [Get started](https://useorigin.app/docs/get-started): Install the Claude Code plugin or run Origin setup for another MCP client, then verify the first memory round trip locally.
- [Daily workflow](https://useorigin.app/docs/daily-workflow): Capture, handoff, distill. The session loop that makes AI work compound across days.
- [Capture quality](https://useorigin.app/docs/capture-quality): What to capture, what to skip, and how corrections, review, distill, and forget fit the memory loop.
- [Review and trust](https://useorigin.app/docs/review-and-trust): How pending captures, revisions, contradictions, quality-gate rejections, confirm, and forget fit before memory becomes trusted context.
- [Core concepts](https://useorigin.app/docs/core-concepts): Memories, pages, entities, sessions, sources. The vocabulary the daemon enforces.
- [Glossary](https://useorigin.app/docs/glossary): Short definitions for Origin terms such as memory, handoff, page, space, daemon, MCP, local index, provenance, supersession, and retrieval metrics.
- [Architecture](https://useorigin.app/docs/architecture): Daemon-first system map: origin-server, origin-core, origin-cli, origin-mcp, origin-types, and the Claude Code plugin.

## Concepts

- [AI work memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-work-memory): What changes when AI sessions can carry context across tools and weeks.
- [MCP memory server](https://useorigin.app/learn/mcp-memory-server): How Origin exposes memory through the Model Context Protocol so any compatible AI client can read and write the same store.
- [Local-first AI work memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/local-first-ai-memory): Data ownership, privacy, control. Why the daemon writes to ~/.origin/ instead of the cloud.
- [Markdown plus local index](https://useorigin.app/learn/markdown-local-index-ai-memory): The storage model. Markdown projection for humans, libSQL index for retrieval, both kept in sync.
- [Distilled wiki pages](https://useorigin.app/learn/distilled-wiki-pages-ai-memory): Why Origin composes atomic captures into source-backed pages with provenance and refreshable revision state.
- [Claude Code memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/claude-code-memory): How Origin works inside Claude Code sessions and how /capture and /handoff fit the work loop.
- [AI agent handoff loop](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-agent-handoff-loop): Session-end discipline that prevents context loss between AI sessions.
- [Origin for Claude Code](https://useorigin.app/learn/origin-for-claude-code): The practical /init, /brief, /capture, /handoff, and /distill loop for Claude Code users.

## Comparisons

- [AI work memory vs knowledge base](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-work-memory-vs-knowledge-base): Documents and notes vs session memory, handoffs, retrieval, and agent reuse.
- [Origin vs Basic Memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/origin-vs-basic-memory): Markdown knowledge base vs AI work-session memory layer. Side-by-side dimensions plus a migration note.
- [Origin vs claude-mem](https://useorigin.app/learn/origin-vs-claude-mem): Observer-style Claude Code memory vs MCP-first cross-tool memory.
- [Origin vs Superlocal Memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/origin-vs-superlocal-memory): Honest LoCoMo comparison (Superlocal currently 4.8 points ahead in zero-LLM config), a 10-second inspectability test, and a two-week parallel-trial protocol for evaluating both.

## Reference

- [Commands](https://useorigin.app/docs/commands): Origin CLI subcommands and the MCP tools the daemon exposes.
- [Claude Code plugin](https://useorigin.app/docs/claude-code-plugin): The richest Origin workflow inside Claude Code: /init, /brief, /capture, /recall, /handoff, /distill, /review, the SessionStart hook, and local memory mode.
- [CLI and service management](https://useorigin.app/docs/cli-and-service): Setup, daemon status, doctor, service install/uninstall, terminal recall/search/store, and MCP client wiring.
- [Updates and uninstall](https://useorigin.app/docs/updates-and-uninstall): Refresh the local runtime, verify with doctor, restart MCP clients, and remove the per-user service safely.
- [Upgrade notes](https://useorigin.app/docs/upgrade-notes): What to rerun, verify, and restart when Origin releases change the plugin, npm setup, MCP connector, daemon service, spaces, or platform support.
- [Packages and registries](https://useorigin.app/docs/packages-and-registries): Which package names mean what across the Claude Code plugin, npm setup, origin-mcp, origin-types, crates.io, and GitHub Releases.
- [Platform support](https://useorigin.app/docs/platforms): macOS, Linux, and Windows support, service managers, data paths, model backend notes, and Docker/VM caveats.
- [HTTP API](https://useorigin.app/docs/http-api): Local daemon surfaces that the plugin, CLI, MCP connector, and local tools call under the hood.
- [API examples](https://useorigin.app/docs/api-examples): Local curl examples for daemon health, setup status, memory store/search, chat context, review, confirm, and page search.
- [Typed clients](https://useorigin.app/docs/typed-clients): When Rust local tools should use origin-types for shared daemon request and response shapes instead of untyped JSON.
- [Spaces](https://useorigin.app/docs/spaces): Separate work, personal, client, and project memory buckets without running multiple Origin installs.
- [Knowledge graph](https://useorigin.app/docs/knowledge-graph): How people, projects, tools, observations, and relations become graph context for retrieval without replacing source-backed memory.
- [Source-backed pages](https://useorigin.app/docs/source-backed-pages): How atomic captures become readable Markdown pages with source memory IDs, revision state, and refresh paths.
- [Import and portability](https://useorigin.app/docs/import-and-portability): Import Markdown-style vault context and keep Origin's projected Markdown artifacts readable outside the daemon.
- [Local git history](https://useorigin.app/docs/local-git-history): Inspect the real git history Origin keeps under ~/.origin/.git for pages, sessions, handoffs, and status artifacts.
- [Models and keys](https://useorigin.app/docs/models-and-keys): No-model local memory mode, optional on-device models, optional Anthropic keys, and the privacy/capability tradeoffs.
- [Advanced retrieval status](https://useorigin.app/docs/advanced-retrieval): Stable hybrid retrieval plus opt-in main-branch retrieval experiments and their release-status caveats.
- [Data and privacy](https://useorigin.app/docs/data-and-privacy): What lives where on disk, what leaves the machine, what does not.
- [Backup and migration](https://useorigin.app/docs/backup-and-migration): Back up readable ~/.origin artifacts and daemon data together, then verify restored installs with doctor and recall.
- [Configuration](https://useorigin.app/docs/configuration): Spaces, MCP client wiring, daemon bind address, local paths, models, and keys without hand-editing the database.
- [MCP clients](https://useorigin.app/docs/mcp-clients): Setup notes for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Claude Desktop, Gemini CLI, and VS Code.
- [Agent profiles](https://useorigin.app/docs/agent-profiles): How Origin attributes captures to Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local scripts, and other clients through source_agent, trust, and enabled state.
- [Troubleshooting](https://useorigin.app/docs/troubleshooting): Common failure modes and the daemon repair paths.
- [Diagnostics and issue reports](https://useorigin.app/docs/diagnostics-and-issue-reports): Commands to run before asking for help, how to separate daemon and MCP failures, and what to redact.
- [FAQ](https://useorigin.app/docs/faq): Short answers about what Origin is, which install path to use, desktop app status, local data, Markdown artifacts, uninstall behavior, and verification.
- [Security and reporting](https://useorigin.app/docs/security): Private vulnerability reporting, redacted bug reports, local security boundaries, and security.txt.
- [Evaluation](https://useorigin.app/docs/evaluation): Retrieval benchmark scope, current LongMemEval and LoCoMo snapshot, and rerun methodology.
- [Desktop app status](https://useorigin.app/docs/desktop-app): Whether the optional GUI is required, how the separate desktop repo fits, and what the daemon still owns.
- [Changelog](https://useorigin.app/docs/changelog): Stable release notes and how to read unreleased main-branch work.
- [Releases and versioning](https://useorigin.app/docs/releases-and-versioning): How release-please, tags, package publishing, npm packages, crates, binaries, and pre-1.0 version bumps work.
- [Roadmap and status](https://useorigin.app/docs/roadmap): Released, on-main, and planned work without confusing roadmap themes for shipped guarantees.
- [Project scope](https://useorigin.app/docs/project-scope): What Origin is for and what it deliberately avoids.
- [Build from source](https://useorigin.app/docs/build-from-source): Source build, workspace crates, platform notes, and contributor checks.
- [Contributing](https://useorigin.app/docs/contributing): Development setup, useful issues, PR process, architecture rules, and license notes.

## Optional

- [llms-full.txt](https://useorigin.app/llms-full.txt): Long-form companion to this file. Contains the full body of every Learn article and Docs page so an LLM can ingest the full corpus in one fetch.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/7xuanlu/origin): Source, releases, issues. Apache-2.0.
- [Origin Claude Code plugin (npm)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@7xuanlu/origin): The Claude Code plugin published from this repo.
- [origin-mcp server (npm)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/origin-mcp): The MCP server binary as a standalone npm package.
- [origin-mcp on crates.io](https://crates.io/crates/origin-mcp): The Rust crate behind origin-mcp.
- [origin-types on crates.io](https://crates.io/crates/origin-types): Shared wire types for downstream tools.
- [License](https://github.com/7xuanlu/origin/blob/main/LICENSE): Apache-2.0.
